architecture_fit.note: Minimal delta from base_model.py: matched-filter score + one hand-set inverse-temperature beta; directly produces the low-entropy single-peaked histogram the goal asks about, on GPU.

baseline_comparison.note: Task's dot-product linear_baseline overlaid everywhere; large canonical sharpness lift (0.61), though since keys/query are unit-norm the contrast is essentially the beta-temperature effect.

faithfulness.note: Synthetic/hand-built and stated as such; the unscaled dot-product baseline acts as the beta=0 ablation, but no separate causal check is proposed beyond it.

operating_range.note: Sweeps the goal's interference axis (cosine 0.0->0.8) and honestly shows sharpness and targeting degrading; beta itself is fixed at one value, not swept.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entirely hand-set — no training, single hand-chosen beta expresses the full mechanism, reproducing the target behaviour.

visual_judgement.note: Histogram panel (mechanism vs baseline, correct key in red) plus sharpness/hit-rate sweep with chance line make the 'sharp and right' vs 'smeared' claim legible at a glance.

visualisation_rationale.note: README clearly ties chart choices to the goal: histogram is the literal object asked about, sweep puts the metric on the y-axis vs rising interference with baseline overlaid.

notes: A clean, honest hand-built attempt: the smallest defensible delta from base_model.py (matched filter + one hand-set inverse-temperature beta) that directly yields the low-entropy, correctly-aimed attention histogram the goal asks about, with the task's dot-product baseline overlaid throughout and well-justified dual visualisations. Limitations are that the mechanism only sharpens (argmax/targeting is identical to the baseline, so the lift is fundamentally the temperature effect) and faithfulness rests on the baseline-as-ablation rather than a dedicated causal probe. Mean of scored items is ~4.0, placing it solidly in 'good'.
